formula_expression: Revenue = Average Selling Price per kg × Total Volume Sold
calculation_sequence: 1. Start from volume by product. 2. Convert to revenue using price per kg. 3. Aggregate by channel and business mix.
business_description: Revenue is driven by selling price and total volume sold across products and channels.

formula_expression: Gross Contribution = Revenue − Variable Cost of Goods Sold
upstream_dependencies: EXE001; BRD004; YLD001; YLD002; PRD001; PRD002; PRD003
calculation_sequence: 1. Begin with revenue. 2. Estimate variable cost from birds, yield and product mix. 3. Subtract from revenue.
business_description: Gross contribution reflects the margin left after direct production cost.

formula_expression: EBITDA = Gross Contribution − Total OPEX − Other Operating Adjustments
calculation_sequence: 1. Start from gross contribution. 2. Subtract operating expense lines. 3. Adjust for non-cash and non-core items if needed.
business_description: EBITDA shows operating profitability before financing and tax.

calculation_sequence: 1. Start from EBITDA. 2. Subtract financing cost. 3. Apply tax to derive after-tax profit.
business_description: PAT represents bottom-line profit after operating and financing costs.

formula_expression: Working Capital Requirement = Inventory + Receivables − Payables
upstream_dependencies: INV001; INV002; INV003; INV004; INV005; WC001; WC002
calculation_sequence: 1. Estimate inventory locked in operations. 2. Add receivables from sales. 3. Subtract supplier payables.
business_description: Working capital requirement captures the cash tied up in the business cycle.

formula_expression: Birds / Day = Required Daily Volume ÷ Average Bird Weight ÷ Yield Efficiency
input_variables: Required Daily Volume; Average Bird Weight; Yield Efficiency
upstream_dependencies: PRD001; PRD002; PRD003; BRD003; YLD001; YLD002
calculation_sequence: 1. Translate demand into required daily processed volume. 2. Adjust for bird weight. 3. Convert to birds per day using yield assumptions.
business_description: Bird requirement reflects the supply of birds needed to meet planned output.

formula_expression: Plant Capacity MT / Month = Planned Throughput Capacity per Month
calculation_sequence: 1. Use the installed plant capacity assumption. 2. Check capacity against demand. 3. Flag expansion if utilization crosses threshold.
business_description: Plant capacity indicates the monthly production volume the plant can handle.

formula_expression: Total Employees = Base Workforce + Required Growth − Automation Adjustment
input_variables: Base Workforce; Growth Requirement; Automation Gain
calculation_sequence: 1. Start from baseline workforce. 2. Add people needed for growth. 3. Reduce for automation productivity gains.
business_description: Total employees reflect the workforce needed to run the plan.

formula_expression: Total CAPEX = Base CAPEX + Expansion CAPEX Triggered
calculation_sequence: 1. Begin with base capex. 2. Add expansion capex if capacity threshold is breached. 3. Aggregate total investment.
business_description: Total capex reflects the investment needed to support growth and capacity.

formula_expression: Total OPEX = Plant OPEX + Commercial OPEX + Admin OPEX
input_variables: Plant OPEX; Commercial OPEX; Admin OPEX
upstream_dependencies: OPX001; OPX002; OPX003; ING_INFL; PACK_INFL; UTIL_INFL; SAL_INFL; MKT_PCT; TRADE_PCT
calculation_sequence: 1. Sum plant, commercial and admin cost lines. 2. Apply inflation and spending assumptions. 3. Aggregate total operating expense.
business_description: Total opex captures the full cost of running the business.
